rows.label: Create route-local conversation session
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Creates a route-local ConversationSession projection with account posture, policy result, and proof-rooted history references.

expandedDetailSections.value: Session identity is local to the route and not a ledger object.
Authenticated account or explicit mock-review posture is required.
Initial source context is limited to source-safe summaries and selector metadata.
Policy admits or denies the session before history is persisted.
Create does not imply an active model stream.
Create history references are replayable without protected source.
Session, account, policy, history, and telemetry roots are required.
Conversation storage is a projection, not global ledger truth.
Protected prompts, source payloads, secrets, and unpaid AssetPack source are forbidden.

rows.label: Restore route-local conversation history
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Restores a ConversationSession and message history projection for the owning account without upgrading it into ledger truth.

expandedDetailSections.value: Restore resolves one conversation id inside the route.
Only the owning account may restore persisted conversation history.
Restored context remains source-safe message and attachment metadata.
Missing or unauthorized sessions fail closed.
Restore does not replay live model output.
History refs preserve message and attachment continuity.
Owner, history, attachment, session, and telemetry roots are required.
Restored history cannot override ledger or Terminal state.
Protected payload classes remain blocked during restore.

rows.label: Create session and stream assistant response
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Creates a ConversationSession from a user write and streams source-safe assistant deltas plus optional execution references.

expandedDetailSections.value: A stream-created session remains scoped to the conversation route.
Authenticated writer identity is required.
Rich inputs are summarized through source-safe token counts and refs.
Empty content and unauthorized writers fail closed.
Stream rows may expose deltas, event ids, and execution refs only.
Assistant completion is persisted as route-local message history.
Rich input, stream, message, session, and telemetry roots are required.
Execution references do not make Conversations the execution authority.
Raw protected prompts and unpaid AssetPack source remain forbidden.

rows.label: Retry stream inside an existing session
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Retries or continues a stream within an existing ConversationSession while preserving prior history roots and policy state.

expandedDetailSections.value: Retry lineage stays under the same conversation id.
Only the owner writer can retry against existing history.
Retry context references prior safe history and the new write summary.
Missing session, empty content, or unauthorized writer fails closed.
Retry stream state is distinct from first stream creation.
Retry references previous restore and new assistant completion refs.
Retry, prior-history, stream, session, and telemetry roots are required.
Retry cannot mutate ledger, settlement, or Exchange state directly.
Protected history is summarized or blocked before retry.

rows.label: Branch route-local conversation history
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Branches a ConversationSession into a new route-local session and copies source-safe message and attachment continuity evidence.

expandedDetailSections.value: Branch creates a new session id with parent linkage.
Only the owner may branch source conversation history.
Branch continuity is message and attachment metadata only.
Missing source or unauthorized owner fails closed.
Branching does not imply streaming.
Copied message and attachment refs preserve branch lineage.
Source, branch, copy, attachment, and telemetry roots are required.
A branch is a route-local projection, not a ledger fork.
Branch copies only allowed source-safe payload classes.

rows.label: Apply shared route-local redaction checkpoint
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Defines the shared redaction checkpoint for route-local mock and live conversation history envelopes.

expandedDetailSections.value: Redaction binds route-local envelopes before serialization.
The route handler applies a policy checkpoint before output.
Only redacted envelope metadata may cross the route boundary.
Forbidden payload classes fail closed and are not retried automatically.
The checkpoint covers both mock and live stream envelopes.
Redaction checkpoints are replayable through source-safe history refs.
Redaction, disclosure, history, serialization, and telemetry roots are required.
Serialization never promotes conversation state into ledger authority.
Protected source, secrets, private wallet material, and unpaid AssetPack source are forbidden.
